w20: Shared-CPU instance with AMD EPYC Genoa architecture, 8 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD, optimized for cost-effective general-purpose computing and CI/CD.
c150: Hetzner Cloud CPX32 is a shared-CPU instance featuring 4 AMD EPYC Genoa cores, 8 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage for cost-effective general-purpose workloads.
c200: Hetzner Cloud CPX32 provides a shared-CPU environment with 4 AMD EPYC Genoa cores, 8 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of SSD storage. It is engineered for balanced performance in general-purpose computing and web serving.
w150: The Hetzner Cloud CPX32 is a shared-CPU instance built on the x86_64 AMD EPYC Genoa architecture. It provides 4 virtual cores, 8 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local SSD storage. The CPU configuration includes 128 KB L1d, 4 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core performance, placing in the top 10 percent for single-core stress tests and top 25 percent in Geekbench single-core scores. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth generally align with average industry standards, though write-heavy large block memory operations demonstrate strong efficiency. This instance operates on a QEMU hypervisor and does not include dedicated GPU resources. Given the hardware profile and benchmark results, the CPX32 is suitable for general-purpose tasks, CI/CD pipelines, and database operations where cost-efficient, balanced compute resources are required.
